bibliographic_data.title: DetNet: A Backbone network for Object Detection
bibliographic_data.abstract: Recent CNN based object detectors, no matter one-stage methods like YOLO [1,2], SSD [3], and RetinaNet [4] or two-stage detectors like Faster R-CNN [5], R-FCN [6] and FPN [7] are usually trying to directly fine-tune from ImageNet pre-trained models designed for image classification. There has been little work discussing on the backbone feature extractor specifically designed for the object detection. More importantly, there are several differences between the tasks of image classification and object detection. (i) Recent object detectors like FPN and RetinaNet usually involve extra stages against the task of image classification to handle the objects with various scales. (ii) Object detection not only needs to recognize the category of the object instances but also spatially locate the position. Large downsampling factor brings large valid receptive field, which is good for image classification but compromises the object location ability. Due to the gap between the image classification and object detection, we propose DetNet in this paper, which is a novel backbone network specifically designed for object detection. Moreover, DetNet includes the extra stages against traditional backbone network for image classification, while maintains high spatial resolution in deeper layers. Without any bells and whistles, state-of-the-art results have been obtained for both object detection and instance segmentation on the MSCOCO benchmark based on our DetNet (4.8G FLOPs) backbone. The code will be released for the reproduction.

core_content.novelty_claim: DetNet is a novel backbone network specifically designed for object detection, which includes extra stages to handle objects with various scales and maintains high spatial resolution in deeper layers.
core_content.key_methodology: DetNet employs a low complexity dilated bottleneck structure to maintain the spatial resolution of features even when including additional stages. It integrates these improvements to keep both high resolution feature maps and large receptive fields, which are important for object detection tasks.
outcomes: Achieved new state-of-the-art results on MSCOCO object detection and instance segmentation track based on a low complexity DetNet59 backbone.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Object detection is one of the most fundamental tasks in computer vision. Recent CNN-based detectors, such as YOLO [1,2], SSD [3], RetinaNet [4], Faster R-CNN [5], and R-FCN [6] rely on ImageNet pre-trained models for image classification. However, there is a gap between the image classification task and object detection due to differences in scale handling and spatial localization requirements. DetNet addresses these issues by incorporating extra stages similar to FPN but maintaining high spatial resolution through a low complexity dilated bottleneck structure. This backbone network achieves state-of-the-art results on the MSCOCO benchmark.
